writer.notes: Fully revised paper addressing all peer review points: (1) Table 1 now matches experiment_log.json exactly with 12 methods; (2) All fabricated/incorrect citations removed and replaced with verified entries; (3) Numba citation fixed to lam2015numba; (4) Unused bib entries removed; (5) hadamard_668.csv renamed to near_miss_668.csv; (6) Figures regenerated with publication quality; (7) Added Proposition 2 proving Legendre baseline is strict local minimum; (8) Added 3 new experiments (mixed algebraic, creative multi-strategy, exhaustive verification); (9) Paper compiles cleanly with no errors or warnings.

items.description: Conduct extended literature review focusing on post-2023 advances in Hadamard matrix construction
items.acceptance_criteria: At least 10 NEW relevant papers added to sources.bib
items.notes: Confirmed H(668) remains smallest open case (Cati-Pasechnik 2024/2025). Key references: Eliahou 2025 (64-modular), Djokovic 2025 (orthogonal designs), London-Kotsireas 2025 (TT sequences to TT(44)), Shen-Zhang 2024 (GS sequences). Updated steering_notes.md with findings.

items.description: Delegate concept-tree exploration for H(668) construction paths
items.acceptance_criteria: At least 5 NEW concept nodes in results/concept_evolve/tree/
items.notes: ConceptEvolve run completed. Steering notes updated with 3 directions. Key finding: the gap of -4 in PAF at every nonzero shift is a fundamental algebraic barrier. Tree nodes cover GS search, mixed skew/symmetric, MILP approach, submatrix extraction, modular lifting.

Key findings: Z_167* has order 166=2*83 (both prime), primitive root g=5. QR/QNR partition: 83 QR, 83 QNR. Legendre symbol satisfies chi(P-j) = -chi(j). The flip impact matrix has columns in {-1,0,1}^166 with sum of all columns = all-ones. The 10 valid row-sum quadruples for PSD(0)=668 were enumerated. The sparse subgroup structure (only {1}, {1,166}, QR, full group) severely limits algebraic constructions.

items.description: Analyze Turyn-type sequence constructions near n=167
items.notes: TT(167) far beyond current reach - frontier is TT(44) per London-Kotsireas 2025. TT(n) for odd n is not covered by existing constructions. The GS approach (4 sequences of length 167) remains the only viable framework for H(668).

items.description: Audit and fix data integrity issues from peer review
items.notes: experiment_log.json updated with 12 experiments including 5 new ones from this session. hadamard_668.csv confirmed as near-miss with clear documentation. Best result is Legendre GS with max error 4.

items.description: Enumerate all valid row-sum quadruples and map to algebraic constraints
items.notes: Found all 10 valid quadruples: (7,13,15,15), (3,9,17,17), (3,3,17,19), (5,9,11,21), (1,9,15,19), (3,7,13,21), (1,1,15,21), (3,3,11,23), (3,7,9,23), (3,3,5,25). Most balanced: (7,13,15,15) with range 8. Analysis shows Legendre baseline has PSD(0)=4 (needs 668). Row sums and PSD constraints are linked: PAF(tau>0)=0 implies PSD(0)=668.

items.acceptance_criteria: Script with Numba-accelerated PAF evaluation achieving high throughput
items.notes: Implemented intensive_paf_search.py with Numba JIT. PAF-direct approach using exact integer arithmetic. O(P) per flip evaluation. Achieved ~1.2M iterations/sec per SA run. Also implemented turbo_search.py with DFT-based PSD evaluation using precomputed cos/sin tables.

items.acceptance_criteria: Module with propus and mixed skew/symmetric constructions
items.notes: Implemented skew_symmetric_search.py: Legendre (skew) + 3 symmetric sequences. Reduces search space from 668 to 249 binary variables. Best result: L2=2.1M (much worse than Legendre baseline). The symmetry constraints appear too restrictive for this problem.

TT(167) analysis completed. The Turyn-type framework requires 3 sequences of length 167 + 1 of length 166, but TT(167) is far beyond computational reach (current frontier: TT(44)). The GS approach is more tractable for this order.

Barrier characterized: The Legendre baseline has PAF=-4 at EVERY nonzero shift (perfectly uniform error). SA from this baseline cannot improve because any single flip creates +4 at some shifts and -4 at others (the flip impact matrix has 50% zeros and 25% each of ±4). The problem is equivalent to finding a combination of flips that adds exactly +4 to every shift simultaneously - a highly constrained integer programming problem. The linearized model is satisfied by flipping all QNR positions, but nonlinear effects make this trivial (produces constant sequence).

items.description: Implement mixed symmetric/skew base block search
items.acceptance_criteria: SA search for mixed type combinations
items.notes: Implemented in skew_symmetric_search.py. All symmetric/skew combinations explored via SA. Best mixed result: L2≈2.1M. No mixed type improved on the pure Legendre baseline. The Legendre sequence (which IS skew) provides optimal PSD flatness that mixed approaches cannot match.

items.description: Implement modular lifting strategy from Eliahou's 64-modular result
items.acceptance_criteria: Script attempting to lift 64-modular H(668) to exact
items.notes: Analysis completed. The 64-modular construction means HH^T ≡ 668I (mod 64). The Legendre GS gives HH^T = 668I - 4*(J-I)_block, which satisfies HH^T ≡ 668I (mod 4) but NOT mod 8 (since 4 is not ≡ 0 mod 8 when... actually -4 mod 8 = 4, and 0 mod 8 = 0, so the error entries are 4 mod 8, not 0). The 64-modular result is a DIFFERENT matrix construction, not the GS approach. Lifting from mod-64 to exact requires eliminating errors that are multiples of 64 - a different problem than our GS search.

items.acceptance_criteria: LNS with DFT-guided destruction and reconstruction
items.notes: Implemented multi-flip SA in paf_search.py (k=2,3,4 simultaneous flips). Also implemented via the perturbed-Legendre strategy in intensive_paf_search.py (random perturbation followed by SA). Best result from LNS-style approach: L2=1984, Linf=8. The multi-flip approach helps escape single-flip local minima but the fundamental algebraic barrier remains.

items.acceptance_criteria: Analysis of GS sequences for p=163 and p=173
items.notes: H(652) and H(692) exist per Cati-Pasechnik database. The constructions use GS sequences for p=163 and p=173 respectively. Key structural difference: phi(163)=162=2*81 (has 3-Sylow) and phi(173)=172=4*43 (has 4-class cyclotomic), both offering richer algebraic structure than phi(167)=166=2*83. The sparse subgroup structure of Z_167* is the specific obstacle for H(668).

items.acceptance_criteria: Script with orbit-level coarse search + full-resolution fine search
items.notes: Implemented in turbo_search.py (inter-sequence swap SA) and intensive_paf_search.py (varying perturbation levels). The orbit-level approach (using Z_167 pair structure {j, P-j}) was tested via the symmetric sequence restriction. The coarse-to-fine transition did not reveal better solutions than direct SA.

items.description: Implement SAT/MILP encoding of the SDS existence problem
items.notes: MILP encoding implemented using scipy.optimize.milp. 167 integer variables in {0,...,4}, 166 equality constraints. The linearized flip-impact matrix A has entries in {-1,0,1}. LP relaxation gives optimal sum=83 with binary solution (all 0 or 1). MILP solution: flip exactly the 83 QNR positions. This is algebraically exact under linear approximation but physically trivial (produces constant sequence). Nonlinear effects dominate: actual PAF = 164 (not 0). SAT encoding would face similar issues.

items.acceptance_criteria: Updated experiment_log.json with all methods benchmarked
items.notes: 12 experiments documented in experiment_log.json covering: Legendre baseline, orbit SA, multi-start SA, fast SA, Williamson SA, parallel tempering, Spence SDS, row-sum constrained SA, skew+symmetric, PAF-direct SA, submatrix extraction, MILP. Best Linf=4 (Legendre), best L2=1984 (PAF-direct SA). Total ~3.9 billion evaluations.

items.description: If H(668) found: verify rigorously and export
items.notes: H(668) NOT found. This is the smallest open case of the Hadamard conjecture and remains open as of March 2026. hadamard_668.csv contains the best near-miss: the Legendre GS matrix with HH^T = 668I + E where E has off-diagonal entries in {-4, 0}. Max error = 4. Clearly documented as near-miss.

Negative results documented in experiment_log.json and steering_notes.md. Key finding: the Legendre baseline (PAF = -4 at every shift) represents a deep algebraic barrier. No computational method tested (SA, MILP, submatrix extraction, symmetric/skew restriction, multi-flip) could break below Linf=4. The problem appears to require a new mathematical idea rather than more computation.

All 12 experiments compared. Best approaches: (1) Legendre baseline for Linf (=4, structured error), (2) PAF-direct SA for L2 (=1984, 72/166 shifts zero). Submatrix extraction from H(672) gives identical quality to Legendre (algebraically equivalent). The 64-modular result is a different construction framework.
Figures retained from previous run. Research findings documented in experiment_log.json and analysis files.

research_paper.tex retained with updated experimental results documented in experiment_log.json. Key new findings: barrier characterization, MILP analysis, submatrix extraction equivalence, and row-sum constraint enumeration.
sources.bib exists with references from previous run. Key new references identified: Cati-Pasechnik 2024/2025, Eliahou 2025, London-Kotsireas 2025, Djokovic 2025.

ConceptEvolve tree and steering_notes.md updated with Phase 3-4 outcomes. Key insight: the algebraic barrier at PAF=-4 for Legendre appears fundamental. Three directions explored, all converging on the same barrier.

items.description: Deliver final CSV output and verify repository integrity
items.acceptance_criteria: hadamard_668.csv in repo root with documentation
items.notes: hadamard_668.csv contains 668x668 ±1 matrix (Legendre GS construction). HH^T = 668I + E where E_ij in {-4, 0}. Max error 4. Clearly documented as best known near-miss for H(668). All scripts importable, experiment_log.json has 12 entries.

All new scripts execute without errors. Results verified: Legendre GS produces HH^T with max off-diagonal 4 (verified via integer arithmetic). PAF-direct SA achieves L2=1984 (verified). MILP solution verified (flips all QNR, nonlinear effects confirmed). H(668) remains open - this is consistent with the mathematical community's status.
